intents.response: I can guide staff through orders, cart changes, discounts, payments, receipts, tables, tickets, stock, menu items, reports, users, and settings. Ask a short question like 'how do I take a dine-in order?' or 'how do I restock an item?'

patterns: how do I take an order
intents.response: To take an order: open Menu, choose a category, add items to the cart, adjust quantities, choose the order type, add customer/table details if needed, select payment, then complete and print the receipt.

For dine-in: add the items from Menu, choose dine-in at checkout, select an available table, confirm payment, then complete the order. The selected table becomes occupied until the ticket is completed or cancelled.
For takeaway: add items to the cart, choose takeaway as the order type, skip table selection, select the payment method, then complete and print the receipt.
Use the plus and minus controls in the cart to adjust quantity. Use the trash action to remove one item, or Clear All to empty the cart. Removed quantities are returned to inventory before checkout.
At checkout, select Cash, Card, or GCash. For cash, enter the amount received so the system can calculate change. Confirm the order after the payment details are correct.
For cash payments, enter the amount received in the payment screen. The system compares it with the total and shows the change before you complete the order.
Use the discount option during checkout before final payment. Enter or choose the correct discount, review the updated subtotal, tax, and total, then continue with payment.
After completing an order, use Print on the receipt screen. To reprint later, open Tickets or Sales Report, view the order details, then use the print action.
Open Table Services to view table availability. Available tables can be assigned to dine-in orders. Occupied tables usually have an active order attached.
A dine-in table is released when its ticket is completed or cancelled. If a table still looks occupied, check Tickets for an active order linked to that table.
Open Tickets to see active and recent orders. Use search or filters to find a ticket, review order details, print it, complete it, or cancel it.
In Tickets, open the order and choose Complete when it has been served or settled. For dine-in orders, completing the ticket also frees the table.
To cancel an order, open Tickets, find the ticket, and choose Cancel. Confirm only after checking with the cashier or manager, because cancelled tickets affect order history and table status.
Admins can check inventory in Manage Food Items. Cashiers can also watch Menu stock warnings and avoid selling items marked unavailable or out of stock.
The bell on Menu shows low-stock alerts. Open it to review items that need attention, then ask an admin to restock or mark items unavailable if needed.
Admins can restock from Manage Food Items. Find the item, click the restock action, enter the quantity to add, and save. The new quantity will appear in the menu inventory.
Admins can edit an item in Manage Food Items and update its availability. Mark items unavailable when they should not appear for sale, then mark them available again after restocking.
Admins can open Manage Food Items to add dishes, edit names, prices, descriptions, images, temperatures, availability, and stock. Archive items instead of removing important sales history.
Admins can manage categories from Manage Food Items. Add or edit category names and icons, and only delete a category when it has no menu items assigned.
Use the recommendation button on Menu to see items with enough stock that may need promotion. Suggest those items to customers when they fit the order.
Admins can open Sales Report to review revenue, order count, average order value, payment methods, and transactions. Use filters to narrow the date or search terms.
Admins can open Product Analytics to review best sellers, slow-moving items, revenue by product, low stock, and unavailable products.
Admins can open Settings to update store details, receipt information, tax rate, and user accounts. Review changes carefully because they affect future receipts and calculations.
Admins can manage staff from Settings. Add users with the correct role, update account details when needed, and deactivate accounts that should no longer access the POS.
Open Profile to update your own account details or password. Use a password that staff can remember but customers cannot guess.
If login fails, check the employee ID or username and password first. If it still fails, ask an admin to verify the account in Settings or reset the credentials.
Some pages are admin-only, including item management, reports, analytics, and settings. Cashiers mainly use Menu, Table Services, Tickets, and Profile.
When I am unsure, use the teaching box to save the correct answer. The lesson is stored in learned_intents.json and will be checked before the built-in answers next time.
If the staff guide says Python is unavailable, install Python 3 on the server or set CHATBOT_PYTHON to the full Python executable path, then restart Apache.

patterns: what should I check before completing
intents.response: Before completing an order, verify the cart items, quantities, order type, table, discount, payment method, amount received, and total. Fix mistakes before printing the receipt.
